BBC One, formerly called BBC One England, is the flagship service of the British Broadcasting Corporation (BBC) in the United Kingdom. The network is available in the UK, and in various other countries, including the Channel Islands, the Isle of Man, the UK's overseas territories, and some parts of Europe.

BBC One is sold by the BBC to ITV
The BBC will be giving up control of one of its most prized assets, its flagship channel BBC One, to ITV, the UK's largest commercial television network. This move is part of a deal to strengthen both companies and create a more level playing field in the television industry.

BBC One is sold by the BBC to a private company for £1.5 billion
The BBC has sold the rights to show the BBC One channel to a private company, Global Media, for £1.5 billion.
In exchange, the BBC will receive a significant share of the revenue generated from the channel, which will be operated independently by Global Media.

BBC One is sold by Sky to Sky News
Sky has announced that it has sold BBC One, the UK's largest TV channel, to Sky News, the 24-hour TV news station owned by the same company.
The deal is expected to bring an end to the long-standing relationship between the two companies, which has seen Sky distribute the BBC's channels to millions of customers.
